purpose: settings.static_graph_facts left at its default True: parse_graph_attributes stamps static=True onto the generated graph-attribute facts (graphml_parser.py:60/90, consumed at load time via pyreason.py:603), so with persistent=false the special/rel bounds escape the per-timestep reset (interpretation.py:260-273 resets only non-static worlds) and the rule regrounds every step — derived(B) appears at t=1 AND t=2, and the regrounding keeps max_rules_time ahead of t so the perfect-convergence exit (interpretation.py:674-680) never fires (get_time 3, three frames per filter probe); the twin static-graph-facts-off loses the t=2 derivation and converges a step earlier (verified at authoring — the harness judges each case within one knob position) — the default-true-static, read-at-load-time and interaction-persistent classes.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md setting:static_graph_facts default-true-static + read-at-load-time + interaction-persistent
